title: [NO TESTS NEEDED] Fix for kernel without CONFIG_USER_NS
body:
I have an embedded device for which the kernel was compiled without `CONFIG_USER_NS`.

Podman 2.2. was working fine with `runc` in root mode, but Podman 3.x is breaking due to a change introduced by https://github.com/containers/podman/commit/ecedda63a6488162c9aad2a99c1ada172340ac7f:

https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/ecedda63a6488162c9aad2a99c1ada172340ac7f/libpod/container_internal_linux.go#L532-L537

The root cause is that the code added above crashing at line 532 due to `/proc/self/uid_map` and `/proc/self/gid_map` not exists in the embedded kernel I'm using.

Here is an example where `runc` handles such kernel without userns support:

https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/c81e273835303664d62c4e958a7f34877c05ed49/vendor/github.com/opencontainers/runc/libcontainer/system/linux.go#L97-L107

This PR follows the same pattern that `runc` uses.

comments.body: LGTM, thx @ntkme I'd like a head nod from @giuseppe
